scenarios.title: Validator routes ollama-served LLM model against ollama envelope, not ml-sidecar envelope

gherkin.given: Config has LLM_MODEL=bug-045-fixture-llm-6gib (profile 6144 MiB), OLLAMA_MEMORY_LIMIT=8G (8192 MiB), ML_MEMORY_LIMIT=3G (3072 MiB), and bug-045-fixture-llm-6gib is in services.ml.model_memory_profiles
gherkin.when: Config.Validate() runs (or the underlying validateModelEnvelopes function)
gherkin.then: Validate returns nil — the ollama bucket accepts the LLM_MODEL because 6144 < 8192, and the ml-sidecar bucket is not checked against LLM_MODEL

scenarios.notes: Maps to AC-5(a). ADVERSARIAL: this case would have FAILED on HEAD de49b2f9 because the single-bucket validator would compare 6000 against MLMemoryLimitMiB=3072 and reject; under the post-fix contract it MUST PASS. This is the case that proves regression detectability per AC-5 § Adversarial signal requirement.

scenarios.title: Validator rejects oversized ollama-routed model with error naming OLLAMA_MEMORY_LIMIT (not ML_MEMORY_LIMIT)

gherkin.given: Config has LLM_MODEL=bug-045-fixture-llm-20gib (profile 20480 MiB), OLLAMA_MEMORY_LIMIT=10G (10240 MiB), ML_MEMORY_LIMIT=3G (3072 MiB)
gherkin.then: Validate returns an error whose message names OLLAMA_MEMORY_LIMIT="10G" and the offending model bug-045-fixture-llm-20gib and its required 20480 MiB; the segment of the error naming the offender MUST NOT mention ML_MEMORY_LIMIT as the offending envelope

scenarios.notes: Maps to AC-5(b). Proves the new correct envelope is named in error messages. On HEAD de49b2f9 the error would name ML_MEMORY_LIMIT="3G" — under the post-fix contract it MUST name OLLAMA_MEMORY_LIMIT="10G".

scenarios.title: Config-generate-time self-consistency check rejects misconfigured smackerel.yaml before env file is emitted

gherkin.given: A test fixture config/smackerel.yaml has llm.model=gemma4:26b (profile 18432 MiB) and deploy_resources.ollama.memory="8G" (8192 MiB)
gherkin.when: `./smackerel.sh config generate --env dev` runs against the fixture
gherkin.then: The command exits non-zero with an error message naming llm.model, gemma4:26b, deploy_resources.ollama.memory, and the profile-vs-envelope gap; no env file is written for the failed environment

scenarios.notes: Maps to AC-5(c). Scope 2 implementation chose Go-side check via cmd/config-validate binary invoked from scripts/commands/config.sh as pre-emit gate (DD-2 atomic-promote pattern). The binary loads the TEMP env file into os.Environ-equivalent, calls internal/config.Load() + Validate(), exits 1 on envelope mismatch. Wrapper test (TestConfigValidate_AC5c_WrapperPropagatesRejection) proves the operator-facing ./smackerel.sh config generate pathway propagates the binary rejection. RED→GREEN proof captured in report.md Scope 2 evidence via controlled git stash push/pop of the wiring in scripts/commands/config.sh.

scenarios.title: Default config/smackerel.yaml is self-consistent — ./smackerel.sh up succeeds on default config out of the box

gherkin.given: A fresh clone of the repo at the post-fix commit, no operator-level config overrides applied
gherkin.when: `./smackerel.sh config generate --env dev` and then `./smackerel.sh up` run sequentially
gherkin.then: Both commands exit 0, all services (smackerel-core, smackerel-ml, ollama, postgres, nats) reach healthy state within standard health-check timeout, and `./smackerel.sh status` reports all services healthy

scenarios.notes: Maps to AC-6 + AC-7. End-to-end out-of-the-box deployment scenario. Post-Scope-3 verification: ./smackerel.sh build + up + status + down all exit 0 on default config/smackerel.yaml (forced fresh build required to invalidate stale Docker layer with single-bucket validateModelEnvelopes; documented in report.md Scope 3 §3.E note). AC-6 close on the FULL integration suite remains pending RQ-QF-001 (2 pre-existing foreign spec 041 QF-connector handshake failures introduced in commit e53ee406).
